channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Takeaways from AP and Lee's report on a pipeline company's lawsuits against Midwest farmers
item.description: The company behind a massive proposed carbon pipeline in the Midwestern U.S. filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Ferkenhoff/Lee Enterprises And Josh Kelety/Ap, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump officials halt $1B in funding for Cornell, $790M for Northwestern
item.description: The move is part of a broader push to use government funding to get major academic institutions to comply with President Donald Trump ’s political agenda.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans are going public with their growing worries about Trump's tariffs
item.description: Republican senators are confronting the Trump administration with a bevy of worries about the economic impacts of the president’s sweeping tariff strategy.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas AG Ken Paxton launches Senate primary challenge against Republican Sen. John Cornyn
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is running for the U.S. Senate in a primary challenge against Republican Sen. John Cornyn, setting up what is likely to become one of the GOP’s most contentious and expensive contests of 2026.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: The Supreme Court has allowed the Trump administration to use a 1798 wartime law, the Alien Enemies Act, to deport Venezuelan migrants it accuses of being gang members, ending the temporary halt on deportations ordered by a federal district judge.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For these seniors, DOGE's affordable housing pause means suffering longer without AC
item.description: The Trump administration froze funding for a program to upgrade aging low-income housing and make it energy efficient. The move threatens hundreds of projects around the country.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida lawmakers want to ease high school graduation requirements
item.description: State lawmakers are working to ease the anxiety of taking a test to help students earn their high school diploma. This includes math and English exams.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China imposes a 34% tariff on imports of all US products starting April 10
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
